title: verbascum trapifolium
intro: Verbascum trapifolium, also known as Two-leaved Mullein, is a biennial plant belonging to the mullein genus, characterized by its large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Verbascum trapifolium are attributed to its ability to soothe and calm the skin, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote healing. In herbal preparations, Verbascum trapifolium is often used in teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall skin health.
